description: Add GET /api/health endpoint for runtime monitoring — returns 200 with JSON status including database connectivity, Next.js status, and process uptime. Returns 503 when PostgreSQL is unreachable.

userStories.title: Create /api/health route handler with basic response
userStories.description: As a DevOps engineer, I need a GET /api/health endpoint that returns a JSON health check response so I can monitor the application's runtime status.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/prisma.ts with a singleton PrismaClient instance (import from @/generated/prisma)
Create src/app/api/health/route.ts with a named GET export
GET /api/health returns 200 with JSON: { status: 'ok', timestamp: ISO-8601 string, uptime: number (seconds since process start), services: { nextjs: 'ok' } }
Response Content-Type is application/json
Endpoint responds in < 500ms
Typecheck passes (pnpm run type-check)
userStories.notes: Create src/lib/prisma.ts as a shared singleton — US-002 will use it for the database check. Prisma schema is empty but the generated client still provides $queryRaw and $connect. Use process.uptime() for the uptime field.

userStories.title: Add PostgreSQL health check via Prisma
userStories.description: As a DevOps engineer, I need the health endpoint to verify database connectivity so I can detect PostgreSQL outages.
acceptanceCriteria: Health endpoint runs SELECT 1 via Prisma ($queryRawUnsafe or $queryRaw) to verify database connectivity
On success: services.database = 'ok', overall status = 'ok', HTTP 200
On failure: services.database = 'error', overall status = 'degraded', HTTP 503
Database check has a timeout so it does not hang indefinitely (< 500ms total response)
Typecheck passes (pnpm run type-check)
userStories.notes: Import the PrismaClient singleton from @/lib/prisma. Use Prisma.$queryRawUnsafe('SELECT 1') wrapped in try/catch. Use AbortSignal.timeout or Promise.race for the timeout guard.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for the health endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automated tests for the health endpoint to catch regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/health/__tests__/route.test.ts
Test: GET returns 200 with status 'ok' when database is reachable
Test: Response includes timestamp (valid ISO-8601), uptime (number >= 0), and services object
Test: GET returns 503 with status 'degraded' when database query throws
Test: services.database is 'error' when database is unreachable
Mock Prisma client to simulate both success and failure cases
All tests pass (pnpm test)
Typecheck passes (pnpm run type-check)
userStories.notes: Mock @/lib/prisma using vi.mock. Use vi.fn() to control $queryRawUnsafe behavior per test. Invoke the GET handler directly — no HTTP server needed.

userStories.title: Archive Ralph run, submit draft PR, and verify CI green
userStories.description: Archive the Ralph run, submit all work as a draft PR with review docs, and confirm CI passes.
acceptanceCriteria: All previous stories have passes: true
Archive .ralph/prd.json and .ralph/progress.txt to .ralph/archives/2026-04-09/api-health/ (plural archives, date and feature as SEPARATE directories)
Create feature branch: feat/4-api-health from agent/next-postgres-shadcn
Push all commits to the feature branch

Create draft PR to development with body containing: Closes #4 on its own line, ## Roadmap Context (Rank: 1, Category: product, Phase: now, Complexity: S, Signal: infrastructure), ## What, ## Why, ## How, ## Manual Review Steps, ## Acceptance Criteria
PR body MUST include 'Closes #4' so the issue auto-closes on merge
PR body MUST include Roadmap Context section matching the pinned roadmap
PR title: feat(#4): add /api/health endpoint for runtime monitoring
Run /ci-status after push — poll until CI completes
If CI fails: read failure logs, fix the issue, push again, re-poll until GREEN
This story is NOT complete until CI pipeline is GREEN
userStories.notes: ALWAYS the last story. Archive MUST happen before PR. CI MUST be green before passes: true. PR body MUST have Closes #4 and Roadmap Context.
